23.reason: The loop condition is incorrect. scanf returns the number of items read (1 on success), not the value read. It will never equal -1, causing an infinite loop. Should check if n == -1 after reading.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The loop condition is incorrect. scanf returns the number of items read (1 on success), not the value read. It will never equal -1, causing an infinite loop. Should check if n == -1 after reading.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The loop condition `scanf("%d",&n) != (-1)` is incorrect. `scanf` returns the number of items read (1 on success), not the value read. This will never equal -1, causing an infinite loop. It should check if the input value is -1, e.g., `while(scanf("%d",&n) == 1 && n != -1)`.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  Condition compares scanf return to -1; intended to stop when -1 is read, but actually stops only on EOF. This may work but is not robust.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  scanf returns the number of successfully read items, not the value read. This condition will not terminate when -1 is input, leading to incorrect loop behavior.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  scanf returns the number of successfully read items, not the input value. It should check if n == -1 to terminate.

31.reason: Incompatible argument type: passing int to function expecting array (int*)

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Incorrect argument. Should pass the array name 's', not 's[100]' which is an element. Change to pop(s).
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  `s[100]` is out-of-bounds array access. The function expects an array argument, but `s[100]` accesses the 101st element (index 100) of an array of size 100. It should be `pop(s)`.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Incompatible argument type: passing int to function expecting array (int*)
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  Compilation error: s[100] is out-of-bounds and type mismatch; should be pop(s).
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Passing s[100] (an integer) instead of the array name s (pointer) causes a type mismatch compilation error.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  s[100] accesses out-of-bounds memory and passes an integer instead of the array. It should be pop(s).
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect function call: passing s[100] (an out-of-bounds element) instead of the array s. Should be pop(s).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Passing s[100] (an out-of-bounds element) instead of the array s to the pop function. Should pass 's' not 's[100]'.]
